---
title: "Parallel execution"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    code-fold: true
    code-summary: "Show code"
---

{{< include _setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _pages-nav.qmd >}}

## Parallel execution

Multiple models, multiple worktrees, in parallel:

- A cheap fast model (Haiku) handles mechanical edits — typos, renames, version bumps.
- A mid-tier model (Sonnet) handles per-file work — debugging, single-feature implementation.
- A larger model (Opus) handles synthesis, architecture, and conversation with you.

Use `git worktree add` so two sessions can work on independent branches without stepping on each other. The orchestrator session decides what to delegate; the worker sessions just do the work.

The cost gradient matters more than people expect. Doing all your work at the top tier when most of it is mechanical is the single biggest budget leak.
